description: Machine-readable inventory of all reviewed and verified indexable pages across the public site. Intended for AI agents and search crawlers that need to know which pages are trustworthy and retrieval-ready.

entries.description: Curated Knowledge Atlas for business, SAP, operations, data, automation, and AI-assisted support concepts.

entries.description: Reference routes from the Knowledge Atlas to profile, services, AI context, and datasets.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.description: Knowledge Atlas maps for process flow, document flow, data dependency, and cross-domain navigation.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.description: A concise process map for order to cash across demand, commitment, fulfillment, billing, and cash.

entries.description: A concise map of the procure-to-pay operating chain from demand to invoice and clearing.

entries.description: Curated SAP support and configuration explanations with conservative boundaries.

entries.title: Agent Usage Guide — How AI Agents Should Use Skill Hub
entries.description: Instructions for AI agents on how to use Skill Hub: choose skills, combine them, ask for missing context, separate facts from assumptions, produce artifacts, and avoid generic framework summaries.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Analysis — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical skills for using AI safely and effectively in professional analysis work: validating AI output, converting notes to requirements, turning problems into backlogs, and defining accountability.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: AI Accountability Working Skill
entries.description: Define who owns AI-generated output, how it is reviewed, and what happens when it is wrong so that AI assistance does not become blame avoidance.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Documentation Review Working Skill
entries.description: Use AI to accelerate documentation review while keeping human judgment in control of every finding, producing a structured review report.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Meeting Synthesis Working Skill
entries.description: Turn raw meeting transcripts or notes into a structured synthesis of decisions, actions, and risks with AI assistance and human review.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Status Reporting Working Skill
entries.description: Draft a structured project status update using AI to synthesize inputs, then review and correct it before sending it to stakeholders.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Test Case Generation Working Skill
entries.description: Generate structured test cases with AI assistance, then validate coverage, edge cases, and traceability before adding them to a test plan.

entries.title: AI Output Review Working Skill
entries.description: Review any AI-generated output for hallucinations, inconsistency, and hidden assumptions before using it for work decisions.

entries.title: AI Prompt Briefing for Work Artifacts Working Skill
entries.description: Write prompts that produce concrete, professional work artifacts rather than generic explanations, by structuring context, constraints, and output format.

entries.title: Business Problem to Backlog Working Skill
entries.description: Transform a vague business complaint into a structured analysis backlog with priorities, owners, and dependencies that a team can execute.

entries.title: Convert Notes to Requirements Working Skill
entries.description: Turn raw meeting notes, interview transcripts, and ticket comments into structured requirements without losing the nuance that matters.

entries.title: Validate AI-Generated Requirements Working Skill
entries.description: Check AI-generated requirements for hallucinations, missing context, fake certainty, and hidden assumptions before they enter a requirements document.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Development — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical skills for using AI safely and effectively in development work: repository context packing, coding-agent task design, code review, tool selection, and MCP workflows.

entries.title: AI Coding Agent Task Design
entries.description: Turn vague work into narrow, testable coding-agent tasks with one objective, relevant files, constraints, validation commands, done criteria, and stop conditions.

entries.title: AI-Generated Code Review
entries.description: Review AI-authored changes before merge: diff inspection, tests, security checks, public/private boundaries, architecture drift, and final merge checklist.

entries.title: AI Tool Selection for Development
entries.description: Choose between Repomix, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Aider, MCP, CodeRabbit, and similar tools based on task type, access level, autonomy, privacy risk, and validation depth.

entries.title: MCP Development Workflow
entries.description: Use MCP and tool servers safely for development workflows: read-only vs write-capable tools, permission boundaries, local filesystem risks, GitHub write risks, and audit logging.

entries.title: Repository Context Packing with Repomix
entries.description: Package selected repo context with Repomix, analyze architecture, discover weak spots, and generate a safe implementation plan.

entries.title: Architecture Skills
entries.description: Practical working skills for solution architects: capability mapping, design review, decision records, non-functional requirements, and system context mapping.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Architecture Decision Record
entries.description: Record why a significant architectural choice was made, what options were rejected, and what consequences follow.

entries.title: Capability Mapping
entries.description: Map what the organization does, what systems support it, and where gaps exist between business functions and technical enablement.

entries.title: Non-Functional Requirements
entries.description: Define measurable quality attributes — performance, availability, security, maintainability — that constrain the solution.

entries.title: Solution Architecture Review
entries.description: Review a proposed solution design against requirements, constraints, risks, and non-functional needs before commitment.

entries.title: System Context Mapping
entries.description: Define what a system does, what it depends on, what depends on it, and where the integration boundaries lie.

entries.title: Artifact Templates — Reusable Templates for Skill Hub Skills
entries.description: Reusable Markdown templates for enterprise consulting and operations work. Data quality rules, root cause analysis notes, stakeholder briefs, architecture decision records, and more.

entries.title: Business Analysis — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for business analysis: requirements elicitation, stakeholder analysis, process analysis, gap analysis, acceptance criteria, and business rules discovery.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Acceptance Criteria Working Skill
entries.description: Define how to verify that a requirement is met, before work starts, so that delivery has an unambiguous pass/fail standard.

entries.title: Assumption and Constraint Mapping Working Skill
entries.description: Surface hidden assumptions, separate them from known constraints, and map the risk of each assumption being wrong before it breaks the project.

entries.title: Business Rules Discovery Working Skill
entries.description: Extract the decision logic that governs how a business operates, document it independently of any system, and identify where current systems enforce, violate, or ignore it.

entries.title: Gap Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Compare what exists today against what is required, classify the difference, and produce a closure plan with effort and ownership.

entries.title: Process Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Document how work actually happens, where it breaks, what systems touch it, and who owns each step — not how it is supposed to happen.

entries.title: Requirements Elicitation Working Skill
entries.description: Turn vague stakeholder complaints into structured requirements with assumptions, constraints, risks, and acceptance criteria.

entries.title: Scope Boundary Definition Working Skill
entries.description: Define what is in scope, what is out of scope, and what sits at the boundary so that projects do not drift into adjacent problems.

entries.title: Stakeholder Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Identify who affects or is affected by a change, what they control, what they need, and how to get reliable information from them.

entries.title: Use Case Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Identify actors, goals, and system interactions to produce structured use cases that reveal missing requirements and boundary conditions.

entries.title: User Story Refinement Working Skill
entries.description: Turn vague user stories into structured, implementable backlog items with acceptance criteria, boundaries, and dependencies.

entries.title: DAMA / Data — Practical Working Skills
entries.description: Practical working skills for data governance, quality root cause, metadata, master data, lineage, reference data, and integration interoperability. Usable tomorrow.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Data Governance Working Skill
entries.description: Diagnose missing data ownership, undefined rules, and unenforced policies. Produce a governance action plan with named owners, decision rights, and enforcement mechanisms.

entries.title: Data Integration & Interoperability Working Skill
entries.description: Diagnose integration failures caused by data mismatch, schema drift, or mapping errors. Define data contracts and validation rules at interface boundaries.

entries.title: Data Lineage Working Skill
entries.description: Trace data from source to consumer. Document transformations, hops, and ownership at each stage. Identify lineage gaps that create audit or trust risk.

entries.title: Data Quality Root Cause Working Skill
entries.description: Trace a data defect from symptom to entry point. Classify the root cause type. Produce a correction plan and a prevention control.

entries.title: Master Data Management Working Skill
entries.description: Map master data domains, identify duplication and fragmentation, define golden record logic, and design replication governance.

entries.title: Metadata Management Working Skill
entries.description: Catalog business, technical, and operational metadata. Identify gaps that block reporting, integration, or AI readiness. Produce a metadata inventory with ownership.

entries.title: Reference Data Management Working Skill
entries.description: Manage code lists, status values, and classification schemes. Prevent drift between systems. Design distribution and change control for reference data.

entries.title: Decision & Validation — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for making evidence-based decisions and validating requirements: trade-off analysis, option comparison, risk dependency mapping, evidence-based recommendations, requirements review, traceability matrix drafting, and test scenario derivation.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Evidence-Based Recommendation Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Write recommendations that state what should be done, why it should be done, and what evidence supports the claim — so that approvers can decide without guessing.

entries.title: Option Comparison Matrix Working Skill
entries.description: Produce a structured comparison of options against weighted criteria so that stakeholders can see why one option is better without relying on narrative bias.

entries.title: Requirements Review Checklist Working Skill
entries.description: Review requirements for completeness, consistency, testability, and feasibility before they are approved for implementation.

entries.title: Risk and Dependency Mapping Working Skill
entries.description: Identify what could go wrong, what depends on what, and which risks are connected so that mitigation plans address root causes instead of symptoms.

entries.title: Test Scenario Derivation Working Skill
entries.description: Derive concrete test scenarios from requirements and acceptance criteria so that testers know exactly what to verify before development begins.

entries.title: Traceability Matrix Drafting Working Skill
entries.description: Map requirements to source, design, test, and release so that every requirement can be traced from origin to verification and no requirement is orphaned.

entries.title: Trade-Off Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Compare competing objectives across multiple dimensions so that decisions are made on evidence rather than on the loudest voice in the room.

entries.title: Framework Map — How Enterprise Frameworks Map to Practical Work
entries.description: A practical map connecting DAMA-DMBOK, BABOK, TOGAF-style architecture, integration architecture, and SAP AMS to usable work skills. Not a framework summary.

entries.title: Skill Hub — Practical Working Skills for Enterprise Consultants and AI Agents
entries.description: A library of practical working skills for enterprise consultants, business analysts, data consultants, solution architects, integration architects, SAP AMS consultants, and AI agents. Not a glossary. Not a framework encyclopedia. Usable work skills.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Integration Architecture — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for designing, owning, monitoring, and troubleshooting enterprise integrations in SAP and mixed landscapes.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: API Integration Working Skill
entries.description: Choose protocols, define contracts, and design resilient synchronous integrations for REST, OData, SOAP, and SAP services.

entries.title: Data Mesh Working Skill
entries.description: Apply data mesh principles to SAP and enterprise landscapes by identifying domains, defining data products, assigning ownership, and choosing integration patterns.

entries.title: Event-Driven Architecture Working Skill
entries.description: Decide whether an event should exist, who owns it, what its contract is, and how failures are monitored and handled.

entries.title: Integration Error Handling Working Skill
entries.description: Design retry, dead letter, and escalation logic so transient failures recover safely and permanent failures escalate correctly.

entries.title: Integration Observability Working Skill
entries.description: Design monitoring and alerting for integrations so failures are detected before business users report them, and diagnostics are fast and repeatable.

entries.title: Interface Ownership Working Skill
entries.description: Assign clear ownership to every interface, document who decides what, and prevent failures from sitting unresolved because no one is responsible.

entries.title: Productivity and Execution Control — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for planning work, clarifying tasks, triaging priorities, escalating blockers, reviewing daily execution, tracking follow-ups, managing delivery checklists, and detecting scope creep.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Blocker Escalation Working Skill
entries.description: Escalate a blocked task to the right person with the right context so the blocker is removed quickly, without blame, and without repeating information.

entries.title: Daily Execution Review Working Skill
entries.description: Run a 10-minute structured daily review that tracks progress, surfaces blockers, and resets priorities so the team starts each day with clarity and alignment.

entries.title: Delivery Checklist Management Working Skill
entries.description: Build and maintain a delivery checklist that confirms every prerequisite is complete, every risk is addressed, and every stakeholder is ready before a milestone is declared done.

entries.title: Follow-Up Tracking Working Skill
entries.description: Track commitments made in meetings and messages so nothing is forgotten, every promise has an owner and a deadline, and accountability is visible.

entries.title: Priority Triage Working Skill
entries.description: Compare tasks by impact, urgency, and effort to decide what to do now, what to do next, and what to decline or defer so capacity is allocated to the highest-value work.

entries.title: Scope Creep Detection Working Skill
entries.description: Detect, document, and challenge scope expansion before it consumes budget and schedule without changing the project charter or approval chain.

entries.title: Task Clarification Working Skill
entries.description: Turn a vague or ambiguous task into a clear, actionable work item with defined inputs, outputs, success criteria, and ownership so the assignee can start immediately.

entries.title: Work Breakdown Planning Working Skill
entries.description: Decompose a project or phase into work packages with clear boundaries, dependencies, and deliverables so the team can estimate, assign, and execute without overlap or gaps.

entries.title: Quality Rules — What Makes a Skill Hub Page Good or Bad
entries.description: Quality criteria for Skill Hub pages. Good page criteria, weak page warning signs, anti-generic writing rules, public-safety rules, agent-readiness rules, artifact-readiness rules, and link quality rules.

entries.title: SAP AMS / Operations — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for SAP Application Management Services and operations: incident triage, root cause analysis, change impact, knowledge capture, and recurring ticket patterns.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Change Impact Analysis — Working Skill
entries.description: Before applying a transport, configuration change, or master data update, know which processes, interfaces, and stakeholders are affected.

entries.title: Incident Triage — Working Skill
entries.description: Classify SAP incidents fast, contain business impact, and route to the right owner without wasting time on premature diagnosis.

entries.title: Operational Knowledge Capture — Working Skill
entries.description: Turn firefighting into reusable knowledge: capture what was done, why it worked, and when it applies.

entries.title: Recurring Ticket Pattern Analysis — Working Skill
entries.description: Find the patterns behind repeated tickets, quantify the cost, and design a permanent fix instead of handling the same symptom forever.

entries.title: Root Cause Analysis — Working Skill
entries.description: Separate symptoms from causes, trace defects to their entry point, and produce a correction and prevention plan for SAP incidents.

entries.title: Skill Page Template — Standard Structure for Skill Hub Pages
entries.description: The standard template for all Skill Hub pages. Defines the required sections, their purpose, and quality criteria. Use this when creating or reviewing skill pages.

entries.title: Systems Analysis — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for systems analysts: mapping entity states, lifecycles, and interface requirements across system boundaries.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Interface Requirement Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Define what data crosses a system boundary, in which direction, under what conditions, and with what quality and error-handling requirements.

entries.title: State and Lifecycle Analysis Working Skill
entries.description: Map the states an entity passes through, the events that trigger transitions, and the conditions that must hold at each stage so that no invalid state reaches production.

entries.title: Testing, QA, and Delivery Validation — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for testing, quality assurance, and delivery validation: test case design, regression planning, defect triage, QA review, deployment readiness, UAT preparation, test evidence review, and release risk assessment.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Defect Triage and Classification Working Skill
entries.description: Classify incoming defects by severity, priority, root cause area, and owner so the right team fixes the right defects in the right order.

entries.title: Deployment Readiness Checklist Working Skill
entries.description: Confirm that every prerequisite for safe deployment is complete: tests passed, rollback plan ready, stakeholders informed, monitoring active.

entries.title: QA Review and Sign-Off Working Skill
entries.description: Run a structured quality gate review before release: verify test coverage, defect status, documentation completeness, and risk acceptance.

entries.title: Regression Test Planning Working Skill
entries.description: Select what to retest based on change risk, impact area, and historical defect density so regression effort is focused and defensible.

entries.title: Release Risk Review Working Skill
entries.description: Assess release risk by weighing open defects, untested areas, dependency changes, and business impact so the go/no-go decision is evidence-based.

entries.title: Test Case Design Working Skill
entries.description: Write step-by-step executable test cases that verify requirements, cover edge cases, and can be executed by testers who do not know the system internals.

entries.title: Test Evidence Review Working Skill
entries.description: Review executed test evidence for completeness, accuracy, and traceability so the test record is audit-ready and credible.

entries.title: UAT Preparation Working Skill
entries.description: Prepare business testers with clear scenarios, test data, environment access, and defect reporting rules so UAT produces valid sign-off.

entries.title: Work Documentation and Handover — Skill Group Index
entries.description: Practical working skills for writing handover notes, decision summaries, action logs, process documentation, incident records, knowledge articles, runbooks, status updates, and lessons learned.
entries.author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau

entries.title: Decision Summary Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Document what was decided, what alternatives were rejected, why, and what the consequences are so future teams do not re-open closed decisions.

entries.title: Handover Note Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Produce a handover package that transfers context, decisions, risks, and open items so the receiving team can continue without starting from scratch.

entries.title: Incident Documentation Working Skill
entries.description: Write an incident record that captures symptom, diagnosis, resolution, and prevention so the next incident is handled faster and patterns are visible.

entries.title: Knowledge Article Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Turn a known fix, workaround, or procedure into a self-service knowledge article that reduces repeat tickets and onboarding time.

entries.title: Lessons Learned Capture Working Skill
entries.description: Capture what worked, what failed, and what to change after a project phase or incident so the organization learns instead of repeating.

entries.title: Meeting Notes to Action Log Working Skill
entries.description: Convert raw meeting notes into a structured action log with owners, deadlines, and dependencies so decisions become executable tasks.

entries.title: Process Documentation Working Skill
entries.description: Document how a business process actually works, who owns each step, what systems are involved, and where it breaks.

entries.title: Runbook Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Write a step-by-step operational runbook that an on-call engineer can follow safely without knowing the system history.

entries.title: Status Update Writing Working Skill
entries.description: Write a project status update that tells the truth about progress, risks, blockers, and next steps without hiding bad news.
